description:
A performance engineer is evaluating a variable-sweep tactical aircraft concept. You need to establish the baseline planform geometry for both the fully unswept (Takeoff/Landing) and fully swept (Supersonic Dash) states, and report the change in exposed lifting area.

Open the baseline model at /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/variable_sweep_base.vsp3.
The model contains a 'Main_Wing' component. Modify it so it has exactly TWO sections (delete any extra sections if present).
Section 1 (Inboard): Leave parameters as is (fixed glove).
Section 2 (Outboard): Variable-sweep panel.

1. TAKEOFF CONFIGURATION (Low Speed)
Set Outboard Panel (Section 2) LE Sweep to 20 deg and Span to 7.5 m.
Save as: /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/vswing_takeoff.vsp3

2. DASH CONFIGURATION (High Speed)
Set Outboard Panel (Section 2) LE Sweep to 68 deg and Span to 4.8 m.
Save as: /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/vswing_dash.vsp3

Finally, run DegenGeom analysis (Analysis > Degen Geom) on BOTH configurations. Find the 'Main_Wing' projected planform area in the generated CSV files. Write these two area values clearly to /home/ga/Desktop/sweep_area_report.txt.
